site.name: Gorix
site.description: An open-source Go framework for APIs, microservices, fintech, AI systems, and production infrastructure.

metadata.title: Gorix — Production-grade Go framework
metadata.description: An open-source Go framework for APIs, microservices, fintech, AI systems, and production infrastructure.

footer.copy: Open-source infrastructure for building production-grade Go systems.

bottom.tagline: Built for APIs, microservices, fintech and AI systems.

page.title: Gorix | Home Page
page.description: Build production-grade Go APIs, microservices, fintech platforms, AI systems, and infrastructure with Gorix.

sections.eyebrow: Open source · Go native · Production focused
sections.title: Build
sections.lead: Gorix is an open-source Go framework for APIs, microservices, fintech platforms, AI systems and production infrastructure.

terminal.title: main.go

sections.title: A strong foundation for the whole production journey.
sections.description: Keep application code structured while Gorix provides conventions for routing, modules, validation, configuration and infrastructure integration.

images.alt: Gorix framework architecture and registered route visualization
Gorix framework performance and observability dashboard visualization

sections.title:
Simple enough to start.
Structured enough to scale.

items.title: Modular by design
items.copy: Compose isolated modules, controllers and services without turning your application into a tightly coupled monolith.

items.title: Performance-minded
items.copy: A Go-native foundation designed for predictable latency, efficient resource use and production observability.

items.title: Microservice ready
items.copy: Build APIs and distributed services around clear boundaries, configuration profiles and pluggable infrastructure.

items.title: Secure defaults
items.copy: Structured validation, controlled configuration and explicit framework conventions reduce avoidable production risk.

items.title: Developer experience
items.copy: Readable project structure, focused APIs and documentation that keep teams productive as systems grow.

sections.title: Clear boundaries from transport to infrastructure.
sections.copy: Gorix promotes explicit modules and focused layers so business logic remains testable, reusable and independent from delivery mechanisms.

sections.title: Take Gorix from repository to running service.
sections.copy: Read the setup guide, inspect the source, and help shape the framework through verified bug reports.

page.title: Bug Report
page.description: Submit a verified bug report to the Gorix maintainers.

hero.title: Report a framework bug.
hero.description: Verify your email, provide a reproducible case, and give maintainers the technical context needed to investigate quickly.

page.title: Enterprise Support
page.description: Request verified enterprise support for Gorix.

hero.title: Get Gorix support for your company.
hero.description: Verify your email, send company details, and share the contact number the team should use for follow-up.

about.title: Build production systems with Gorix and Gromosome.
about.description: Gromosome builds open-source and enterprise-grade infrastructure for modern software teams. Gorix is part of that ecosystem: a Go-native framework focused on clear application structure, production readiness, and scalable backend development.

page.title: Code Challenge
page.description: Submit an innovative public GitHub project built with Gorix.

hero.title: Share an innovative Gorix project.
hero.description: Submit a public GitHub repository after Firebase email verification. Approved projects appear on the leaderboard by Firestore score.

shared.configurationHelp: Copy .env.example to .env.local and add your Firebase configuration.
shared.requiredNotice: Required fields are marked. Reports are stored securely through the server API.

githubOAuth.title: Sign in with GitHub to continue.
githubOAuth.errorMessage: Unable to sign in with GitHub.
githubOAuth.footnote: GitHub OAuth helps verify project ownership context and reduce spam.

staticExport.title: Submissions are unavailable on GitHub Pages.
staticExport.description: This page is deployed as a static site. Bug reports and challenge submissions require the Next.js API backend, so use the full application deployment for this workflow.

bugReport.oauthDescription: Sign in with GitHub before submitting a reproducible Gorix framework bug.
bugReport.successMessage: Your bug report was submitted to the Gorix maintainers.
bugReport.submitError: The report could not be submitted.

sections.title: Issue classification
sections.description: Help maintainers triage the report correctly.

sections.title: Environment
sections.description: Version and platform details are required.

sections.title: Behavior
sections.description: Provide enough detail for another engineer to reproduce it.

bugReport.consent: I confirm this report does not contain secrets or personal data and the maintainers may review it with my authenticated GitHub account.

codeChallenge.oauthDescription: Sign in with GitHub before submitting a public repository that uses Gorix in an innovative project.
codeChallenge.successMessage: Your project was submitted. It will be listed after our team verification
codeChallenge.submitError: The project could not be submitted.
codeChallenge.submitNotice: The Submissions will be verified by the Gorix team.
codeChallenge.consent: I confirm this is a public GitHub repository and the Gorix team may review it with my authenticated GitHub account before listing.

projects.title: Code challenge leaderboard.
projects.description: The verified projects by the Gorix team.
projects.emptyDescription: After approval by the Gorix team, your projects will appear here.
